Fix any failures; document if a golden is flaky or needs fixture refresh.

name: Light/dark mode: auto-detection + light/dark/auto toggle
description:
GOAL
1. Auto-detect the OS color scheme and follow it.
2. Add a user-facing toggle with three states: Light, Dark, Auto.
3. Persist the selection end-to-end — from the web UI, through a Tauri command, into on-disk storage that survives app reload and relaunch.

Auto-detection is already implicit — every *.css.ts uses `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` blocks. There is no theme store, no toggle, and no override mechanism yet.

PERSISTENCE (firm requirement)
The selected mode (Light / Dark / Auto) must round-trip:
- UI sets the mode -> invoke a Tauri command -> Rust writes to disk.
- App launch -> Rust reads from disk -> UI hydrates from that value before first paint (to avoid a flash of the wrong theme).
- Where exactly the value lives in src-tauri is open — likely a small persisted store next to the existing workspace storage; whatever fits cleanly with the current Rust architecture. Confirm the choice with me before implementing.
- localStorage alone is not sufficient; it can supplement (e.g. as a same-session cache for fast first paint) but the source of truth lives on disk via Tauri.

WORK WITH ME
Before writing code, propose an approach for these and get my sign-off:
- Override strategy for CSS. The current `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` blocks only respond to the OS. Options worth comparing:
  • A `data-theme` (or class) attribute on `<html>` plus selectors like `[data-theme='dark'] &` alongside the existing media query — minimal restructuring, but every dark block needs the extra selector.
  • Vanilla Extract themes / contracts (createTheme / createThemeContract) — cleaner long-term, larger refactor of tokens.ts and every consumer.
  • CSS custom properties driven by JS — flexible but moves styling out of the VE model the codebase prefers.
- Where in src-tauri the persisted value lives, and the shape of the get/set commands.
- Where the toggle lives in the UI. AppTopBar feels natural; confirm before placing it.
- "Auto" semantics. Follow `prefers-color-scheme` live (listen to changes) vs. snapshot at app start. Live is the better UX but slightly more code.

CONSTRAINTS
- Vanilla Extract only; no hex outside tokens.ts.
- Rust commands return Result with explicit errors (fail fast — do not panic on user paths).
- Accessibility: the toggle should be keyboard-reachable and have an obvious label per state.
- Avoid flash-of-wrong-theme on launch: resolve the saved preference before first paint.

ACCEPTANCE
- Toggle with three states works: Light forces light, Dark forces dark, Auto follows OS (and updates live if OS preference changes).
- Selection persists across full app restart (not just page reload), proven by quitting the app and reopening.
- No flash of the wrong theme on launch.
- All existing components still look right in both modes; no orphaned `@media` blocks that ignore the override.
- No new lint / typecheck / fallow findings; clippy + rustfmt clean.
result: Shipped Light/Dark/Auto theme end-to-end: Tauri theme_mode in settings.json (get/set commands), index.html bootstrap + localStorage cache for first paint, data-theme + darkScheme CSS migration off prefers-color-scheme, AppTopBar cycle toggle with native window theme sync, and auto mode listening to OS changes. Verified check/typecheck/fallow/clippy locally.

name: macOS release workflow (GitHub Releases)
description:
GOAL
Add `.github/workflows/release-macos.yaml` that builds the Tauri app for Apple Silicon and attaches bundles to a GitHub Release — not on every push to main (macOS minutes are expensive).

TRIGGER (pick one clear model, document in the workflow header)
Prefer the GitHub Releases UI flow:
- `on.release.types: [published]` — run when a release is published (tag already exists).
- Checkout `github.event.release.tag_name` so the build matches what was released.
- Upload with `tauri-apps/tauri-action` using `releaseId: ${{ github.event.release.id }}` (attach assets to the release the user created; do not create a competing release from scratch unless we document why).

Optional: `workflow_dispatch` for a packaging smoke build (artifacts only, no release row).

Do NOT wire `push.branches: [main]` for full macOS bundles — too costly for routine merges. CI already covers main on Linux.

BUILD
- `runs-on: macos-latest`, `args: --target aarch64-apple-darwin`
- `tauriScript: bun run tauri` (project CLI, not global)
- Reuse composite actions: `install-rust` (cache on), `install-bun-and-dependencies`
- `rustup target add aarch64-apple-darwin` (explicit target, predictable bundle paths)
- Unsigned for now; comment points at https://v2.tauri.app/distribute/sign/macos/ and APPLE_* secrets for later

HOUSE STYLE
- Pin actions by SHA (match `continuous-integration.yaml`)
- Comments: why over what, Greg tone (short, no semicolon chains) — see recent workflow comment pass on the deleted file for vibe
- `permissions.contents: write` only on the job that talks to Releases API
- `concurrency` per ref, `cancel-in-progress: false` (avoid half-uploaded releases)

ACCEPTANCE
- Publishing a GitHub Release (with a `v*` tag) runs the workflow and attaches `.dmg` / macOS bundle assets to that release
- No macOS release workflow on ordinary merges to main
- `workflow_dispatch` still works for manual artifact checks
- Workflow file deleted in resizable-pane branch — this task reintroduces it with the strategy above
result: Shipped via PR #20 (builds). .github/workflows/builds.yaml builds the Tauri app and attaches bundles to GitHub Releases. Subsequent fixes landed in 84714fb (build fix) and f6a0017 (macOS dmg fix). Follow-up: app icon for the built binary is tracked separately.

name: Render recipe markdown in app
description:
## Goal
Rendered instructions on preview + detail so Greg can **audit** steps/notes before confirm and read saved recipes comfortably (parent `jvprxjqg`).

## Today

## Work
1. Allowlisted markdown renderer (e.g. react-markdown) for read mode on detail; edit stays textarea for now.
2. Same rendering on **query preview** instructions (not only debug panel).
3. Sanitize/allowlist — imported content is external.

## Acceptance
- Lists/headings/steps readable; preview supports confirm/discard decision.
result: Recipe markdown renders in app (instructions, notes, detail view).

name: Validate project paths before registration
description: Add a validate_project(config_path, storage_path) Tauri command on the Rust side. Two checks: (1) storage directory contains tasks.jsonl (fast filesystem gate); (2) trial dex list --json call with the provided paths succeeds. Return a clear error message to the UI if either fails. UI should call this before add_project so the user gets feedback before anything is saved.
result: Added validate_workspace Rust command that checks for tasks.jsonl presence and runs a trial dex list --json, then wired it into the frontend registration flow before add_workspace

name: File watcher + tasks-changed event
description: Watch .dex/ with notify + notify-debouncer-mini (~30ms window). On change, emit a signal-only Tauri event (no diff payload). Frontend receives the event and re-calls dex list --json to patch its state. No diffing, no backpressure handling, no frontend debounce — keep it simple. Watcher runs for the lifetime of the app process (inherently only active while gdex is open). Future consideration: pause the watcher when the window is not visible/focused to avoid unnecessary work — worth investigating how macOS surfaces window visibility to Tauri before implementing.
result: Added TaskWatcher (notify-debouncer-mini, 30ms debounce) that watches each startup-registered project storage path and emits tasks-changed with project_id.

name: Batch pick: golden harness script
description: Add bun run spoonacular:ingredient:pick:batch:golden. Loads batch manifests, one Claude call per batch, asserts against inherited *.expected.json. Done when both batch cases pass.
result: spoonacular:ingredient:pick:batch:golden passes both batch cases (inherits per-ingredient expected).

name: Surface task ID with quick copy in detail panel
description: The task ID (e.g. 'xrc0mxfb') should be visible and instantly copyable in the task detail panel. Clicking it copies to clipboard — no extra steps. Consider subtle but accessible presentation: small monospace ID near the task name, copy icon or click-to-copy behavior with brief visual feedback (e.g. checkmark flash). This is foundational for the agent instruction snippets feature (oobeapzz) — the ID is what agents need to reference dex tasks.
result: Added a subtle monospace task ID button in the status row of TaskDetailHeader that copies the ID to clipboard on click, showing a '✓ Copied!' label for 1.2 seconds with accent color, then resetting cleanly via a ref-tracked timeout that is cleared on unmount.

name: UI: ingredient name vs amount vs note
description:
## Goal
Preview and saved-recipe views make it **easy to audit** ingredient lines before saying “yes” — supports the human gate in parent `jvprxjqg`.

## Why
Greg needs to spot wrong names, merged notes, and bad qty/unit at a glance. If the preview is muddy, we’ll confirm bad data into Turso.

## Today
- Query + detail: `name — qty unit note` as one gray suffix; notes look like part of the name.

## Work
1. Shared line layout: **name** prominent; **qty + unit** secondary; **note** visually distinct (sub-line / italic — match app).
2. Apply on `/query` preview and `/recipe/$recipeId`.
3. Payload unchanged (name, quantity, unit, note still separate in API).

## Acceptance
- Notes never read as the canonical ingredient name.
- Greg can review a full ingredient list without opening raw JSON/markdown.
result: Shared RecipeIngredientLine: bold name, secondary qty+unit, italic note on query preview and recipe detail.

name: Collapsed sidebar: deterministic per-workspace visual distinction
description:
GOAL
When the workspace sidebar is collapsed, each item should be visually distinguishable at a glance — not just by its initial letter (current behavior in src/components/WorkspaceSidebar/CollapsedWorkspaceList.tsx). Derive the distinction deterministically from the workspace name so the same workspace always looks the same across sessions and machines.

CURRENT STATE
- Collapsed item = 2rem square, first letter of name, single accent color for active/hover (workspaceSidebar.css.ts: collapsedWorkspaceSquare / ...Active).
- Workspace shape: { id, name, configPath, storagePath } (src/schemas/workspace.ts).
- Palette today is a small curated set in src/styles/tokens.ts — no rainbow swatch palette exists yet.

APPROACH (to decide during the work)
- Deterministic hash of `workspace.name` -> index into a small palette (and/or icon set). Simple string hash (FNV-1a or djb2) is plenty; document the choice inline.
- Prefer CSS-only application: compute a class or CSS variable per item, drive bg / border / accent text via that. JS does the hash; CSS does the painting. Define swatches as a Vanilla Extract style map in workspaceSidebar.css.ts (or a new module under src/styles/) using palette tokens — do not hard-code hex outside tokens.ts.
- Keep light + dark mode working; pick swatches with enough contrast against both surface and surfaceDark, and against the active border.

OPEN DESIGN QUESTIONS (resolve before/during implementation)
1. Color vs icon vs both? Start with color (smallest change, fewest assets). Revisit icons only if color alone is not distinct enough.
2. Should workspace names be required to be unique? Today nothing enforces it — if two workspaces share a name they would hash to the same swatch. Decide whether to (a) enforce uniqueness in the add-workspace flow (src/components/WorkspaceSidebar/AddWorkspacePanel.tsx + the Rust command), or (b) accept rare collisions and rely on the letter + tooltip to disambiguate.
3. Neighbor-collision avoidance? With ~6–10 swatches and a hash, adjacent items can occasionally land on the same color. Options: ignore it (simplest), nudge to the next swatch when a neighbor collides (small post-pass in CollapsedWorkspaceList), or pick a palette size that makes collisions visually fine. Default to "ignore unless it looks bad in practice."

CONSTRAINTS / STYLE
- Vanilla Extract only — no inline style objects beyond a single CSS-variable assignment if needed for dynamic indexing.
- Functional helpers with early returns; no forEach.
- Keep the hashing helper small and pure, colocated with CollapsedWorkspaceList or under src/lib/ if reused elsewhere. JSDoc one-liner on the export.
- Respect existing accessibility: aria-label and title already carry the full name; the color is decorative — do not remove the letter.

ACCEPTANCE
- Each collapsed sidebar item has a stable color derived from its name.
- Light + dark modes both look intentional; active state still reads as active.
- No new lint / typecheck / fallow findings; no hex literals outside tokens.ts.
- A short note in AGENTS.md (or this task's followup) if we landed on a convention worth remembering (e.g. "palette swatches live in styles/workspaceSwatches.css.ts").
result:
Collapsed sidebar: deterministic per-workspace pastel letter colors.

- FNV-1a hash of trimmed/lowercased workspace name → index into palette in tokens.ts
- VE letter class map (workspaceSwatches.css.ts) + workspaceSwatch.ts helper
- CollapsedWorkspaceList applies swatch classes on initials span (avoids global button color)
- add_workspace rejects exact duplicate names (trimmed); duplicate check trims stored names too
- Co-located workspaceSwatch.test.ts; Vitest wired as first test file

name: App feature: paste full recipe + URL (alternate query tab)
description:
Goal: add an alternate mode in the real app where a user pastes an entire recipe plus the source URL, and we run the same AI extraction flow.

Requirements:
- UI: new tab/option on the main query surface
- Input: user provides (a) the full recipe text and (b) the URL (used to dedupe / prevent rescans)
- Server: reuse the same extraction pipeline as URL mode, but pageDigest comes from pasted text (normalized/truncated similarly)
- Safety: still enforce URL normalization/dedupe checks; no unauthenticated access

Done when:
- User can choose URL mode or Paste mode
- Paste mode submits {url, pastedText} and produces the same RecipeQueryResult shape
- Existing URL mode behavior unchanged
- Basic fixture-based test/harness exists for pasted text normalization + extraction prompt input (no live network needed)
result: Paste recipe mode on /query: shared Claude extraction via prepareRecipePageDigestFromPastedText; server accepts pastedText; URL vs Paste UI with textarea.

name: Fallow: adopt + manage findings
description:
Current state (already done)
- fallow installed (devDependency): fallow@2.66.1
- package.json: trustedDependencies includes fallow
- .fallowrc.json exists (strict JSON)
- .gitignore includes .fallow/
- CI: .github/workflows/continuous-integration.yaml has job fallow using fallow-rs/fallow@v2 with command=audit and checkout fetch-depth=0

Observed findings (from bun run fallow)
- Dead code: 31 issues (26 unused exports, 1 unused type, 1 unused dependency, 3 test-only production deps)
- Dupes: 10 clone groups (~8.3% dup rate)
- Health: 32 above threshold
- Deps detail (from fallow dead-code --unused-deps):
  - Unused dependency: @tanstack/react-router-ssr-query
  - Test-only production deps (candidate move to devDependencies): @cloudflare/vite-plugin, @tailwindcss/vite, drizzle-kit

Scope / intent
- Primary: verify CI audit behavior and fix safe deps hygiene.
- Do NOT: large refactors for dupes/health yet (defer unless explicitly selected).
- Severity: decide whether unused-dependencies should stay warn vs become error after signal is trusted.

Commands (local)
- bun run fallow
- bunx fallow dead-code --unused-deps
- bun run build
- bun run typecheck
- bun run test

Acceptance criteria
- CI fallow job runs and gates via fallow audit as intended (new-only by default).
- Dependency decisions are made and validated via build/typecheck/test.
- If removing/moving deps: bun run build + typecheck + test still pass.
result: Fallow adoption complete: CI audit + baselines versioned, ratchet plan in .fallowrc.jsonc, dupes/health baselines committed, deps hygiene done. Remaining dupes/complexity grandfathered; optional dedup pass deferred.

name: Script/prod parity audit: unify helpers + assurance
description:
Goal: ensure script harnesses and production code paths share the same helpers and that we have confidence prod behavior matches what scripts test.

Scope:
- Inventory helper functions used by scripts (URL fetch/digest, normalization, schema validation, prompt assembly)
- Compare to production entrypoints and ensure a single source of truth (no drift)
- Add lightweight verification harnesses that run without live network where possible (fixtures-based)

Done when:
- Documented map of script->prod helper usage
- Any duplicated logic is refactored to shared helpers (or explicitly justified)
- At least one fixture-based check validates prod helpers produce the same intermediate artifacts as scripts (e.g. pageDigest normalization)

Accepted out of scope for now: live fetch/Spoonacular search, full enrich E2E, DB resolution, prod fallbacks (goldens target AI behavior only).

name: Task list: right-click copy agent prompt
description:
Follow-up to the detail-panel agent prompts (parent oobeapzz).

Add a context menu when right-clicking tasks in the task list (and possibly elsewhere later). Initial item(s) mirror quick prompts from the parent feature — e.g. "Copy \`start task\` quick prompt" — copying the interpolated snippet to clipboard with the same brief feedback pattern as task ID copy.

Scope for v1:
- Right-click on a task row opens a small context menu
- At least one action: copy a "start task" (or kick off) style prompt for that task's id + active workspace
- Structure so more menu items can be added later without rework

Out of scope here: full parity with every snippet in the detail-panel dropdown; shared snippet definitions between panel and menu can be a small refactor when both exist.

Depends on: parent task landing shared snippet text / interpolation helpers (or duplicate minimally for v1).
result: Task list right-click context menu: four quick prompts, copy on click, portal + menu styling.

name: Collapsed sidebar: icon strip mode
description: The projects sidebar stays and already supports collapse/expand (in progress). This task is about making the collapsed state a good experience. When collapsed, the sidebar shows a vertical strip of rounded squares — one per project — each containing a single letter representing the project name. Clicking a square switches the active project (same as the expanded state). A plus button at the bottom adds a new project. Between the plus button and the expand icon, the project squares scroll vertically (invisible scrollbar, no gutter) when the list overflows the window height. The task list sidebar + detail panel layout is already done and not in scope here.
result: Collapsed sidebar: scrollable letter squares (CollapsedProjectList), expand header, plus opens add flow by expanding with register form; hidden scrollbar strip; shared collapse/add icon styles.

name: Middle column (task list) must scroll when content overflows
description:
BUG
The middle column in the workspaces layout (the task list at the current zoom level) does not scroll. When there are more tasks than fit vertically, items below the fold are clipped and unreachable. Reproduced visually with the gdex workspace.

CAUSE
- src/components/TaskBoard/TaskBoard.tsx renders <div class={styles.board}> with `flex: '0 1 13rem'`, `minHeight: 0`, `overflow: 'hidden'` (src/components/TaskList/taskList.css.ts:9-19).
- The child <ul class={styles.list}> (taskList.css.ts:21-28) has no overflow and no flex sizing, so when content exceeds the board height it is clipped instead of scrolling.

FIX
Apply the same pattern that already works for the sidebar in src/components/WorkspaceSidebar/workspaceSidebar.css.ts:35-43 (`sidebarBody`):
  - flex: 1
  - minHeight: 0
  - overflowX: hidden
  - overflowY: auto

Two reasonable shapes — pick whichever keeps the CSS simplest:
1. Add `flex: 1`, `minHeight: 0`, `overflowY: 'auto'`, `overflowX: 'hidden'` directly to `styles.list` in taskList.css.ts. Smallest change.
2. Introduce a scroll wrapper div inside `TaskBoard` (between `styles.board` and `<TaskList>`) carrying those properties, so `styles.list` stays purely about layout. Cleaner separation if TaskList ever renders non-<ul> content.

Default to option 1 unless something pushes us to option 2.

CONSIDER
- Match the collapsed-sidebar scrollbar-hidden pattern (`scrollbarHidden` in workspaceSidebar.css.ts:68-76) only if a native scrollbar looks bad inside the narrow column; otherwise let it show.
- Verify behavior at both `flex: '0 1 13rem'` minimum and when the detail panel is open / closed — no horizontal scrollbar, no layout reflow on the right column.
- Empty / single-task states should not introduce a stray scrollbar.

ACCEPTANCE
- With many tasks in the middle column, the list scrolls vertically inside the board; the breadcrumb and surrounding chrome do not.
- Light + dark mode both look intentional.
- No new lint / typecheck / fallow findings.
result: Fixed: middle TaskList column now scrolls when tasks overflow; applied the WorkspaceSidebar sidebarBody pattern (flex: 1, minHeight: 0, overflowY: auto). Verified check/typecheck/fallow pass.

name: Prod: batch ingredient picks in app
description:
Wire batched Claude ingredient picks into production enrichment (not golden scripts only).

Architecture (keep layers):
- lib/ai/spoonacular/* — Claude pick helpers + schemas (single + batch); no DB/auth
- lib/spoonacular/ingredient-nutrition.ts — Spoonacular search + pick orchestration
- fn/* — thin server fns (auth, env, DB); call lib/spoonacular only
- routes — call server fns only; no inline AI
- test:ai:* scripts — call same lib/ai helpers as prod

Routing rule (important):
- pickSpoonacularIngredientCandidateWithClaude / single-item orchestration: ONLY when there is exactly 1 ingredient to pick (N === 1).
- 2+ ingredients: always use pickSpoonacularIngredientCandidatesBatchWithClaude (chunk ~5). Do NOT loop single picks for multi-item work.
- Batch failure for N > 1: retry batch or fail per item — do NOT silently fall back to N single Claude calls (that defeats the purpose).

Scope:
- Plural enrich API for multi-ingredient flows (recipe import, bulk resolve)
- Keep tryEnrichNewIngredientNutritionFromSpoonacular for the single-ingredient case (existing ingredient page path)
- Chunk batch picks at ~5; reuse batch helper + aligned system prompt
- No duplicated pick logic in routes/scripts

Done when:
- Prod multi-ingredient paths use batch helper only; single helper only when N === 1
- test:ai golden harnesses still pass
- No new Claude prompts in fn/ or routes/
result: Prod uses pickSpoonacularIngredientsWithClaude (N=1 single, N≥2 batch chunks of 5) from ingredient-nutrition → resolveIngredientsAgainstDb on new recipe ingredients. Single-ingredient page uses tryEnrichNewIngredientNutritionFromSpoonacular (delegates to plural API). No pick loops for multi-item. CI green.

Replace interim / → GitHub profile redirect with a fuzzy-find UI over Greg's GitHub projects.

Requirements:

name: Resizable divider between task list and task detail (with @preact/signals)
description:
GOAL
Add a draggable column divider between TaskBoard (middle column) and TaskDetail (right pane) inside WorkspaceMain. Width should clamp to sensible min/max, persist across app restart, and feel native — no flicker, no re-render storm during drag, full keyboard support.

DECISION: BESPOKE IMPLEMENTATION
We are building this ourselves. No npm dependency for the resizer — not react-resizable-panels, not allotment, not anything similar. The implementation is ~80 lines of native PointerEvents + CSS Grid + a typed @property + @preact/signals. Reasons, explicit so we do not relitigate:
- Vanilla Extract integration with a library that generates its own classNames is ongoing friction.
- The library's biggest "free" win is persistence — and we need Tauri-on-disk persistence, not browser storage, so that win evaporates.
- One divider does not justify a dependency. The codebase aesthetic (AGENTS.md) leans small and owned.
- The Preact-compat shim is the codebase's first reach for it; precedent matters.

WHEN TO REVISIT THIS DECISION
Migrate to react-resizable-panels (and delete the bespoke Resizer) the FIRST time any of these lands:
- A second resizable boundary (vertical split inside TaskDetail, third column, nested panel groups).
- A need for snap points, collapse-to-min with imperative control, or panel reordering.
- An accessibility audit finding we cannot fix in <1 day inside the bespoke handler.
Do NOT duplicate the bespoke Resizer for a second boundary. Add a comment in the Resizer file pointing at this clause so future-us sees the trigger.

CHOSEN APPROACH (signals-first)
This task adopts @preact/signals for the ephemeral drag state. Signals are not used anywhere else in the codebase yet — this is the first foothold and worth a short ADR note (in AGENTS.md or a follow-up) so the pattern is intentional, not accidental.

Why signals here (vs. useState / Zustand):
- Drag updates fire ~60 Hz. useState would re-render the component every frame; Zustand would re-render every consumer of the width.
- Signals give fine-grained reactivity: only the bit of DOM that reads the signal updates. The cursor / aria-valuenow / data-dragging flag can bind to the signal without re-rendering TaskBoard or TaskDetail.
- The committed (post-drag) width still lives in Zustand and persists via Tauri — signals own the live drag, Zustand owns the durable state.

WORK WITH ME
Confirm with me before implementing:
- The signals adoption itself — happy to make this the first signals usage, but I want to acknowledge it.
- Persistence layer: this should reuse whatever prefs mechanism comes out of task 1hls8kup (theme persistence). If 1hls8kup has not landed yet, decide whether to (a) block on it, (b) introduce a minimal prefs command we both consume, or (c) ship width-in-memory first and persist later.

TECHNICAL RECIPE (2026)
Layout in src/views/views.css.ts — switch .workspaceMain from flex row to CSS Grid:
  gridTemplateColumns: 'var(--task-board-width, 13rem) auto 1fr'
  (auto = the resizer handle column; 1fr = TaskDetail)
Drop the hard-coded `flex: '0 1 13rem'` / `maxWidth: '16rem'` from `.board` in src/components/TaskList/taskList.css.ts; width is owned by the grid container var.

Register the custom property so it is typed and animatable:
  globalStyle('@property --task-board-width', { syntax: '<length>', inherits: false, initialValue: '13rem' })

Resizer component (new — src/components/WorkspaceResizer/ ...):
- role=\"separator\", aria-orientation=\"vertical\", aria-valuenow/min/max, aria-controls=\"task-board\", tabIndex=0
- onPointerDown -> setPointerCapture, capture startX + startWidth into signals
- onPointerMove -> compute clamped width, write directly to container.style.setProperty('--task-board-width', \`\${w}px\`) via a ref. NO setState, NO Zustand write during drag.
- onPointerUp -> release capture, commit final width to Zustand, debounced Tauri persist
- Keyboard (APG splitter pattern): Arrow keys nudge ±8px, Home/End jump to min/max, Enter cycles snap positions (e.g. default 13rem).
- touchAction: 'none' on the handle; user-select: none on body during drag.

Use @preact/signals for ephemeral drag state:
- isDragging signal -> drives cursor / data attribute for styling, without re-rendering panes.
- liveWidth signal -> updated on each move so aria-valuenow stays accurate; reads cheap because only the separator binds to it.

Add container queries on the panes (src/components/TaskBoard/* and src/components/TaskDetail/*):
- containerType: 'inline-size' on the pane roots
- @container (max-width: 9rem) { /* compact mode for TaskBoard */ }
- @container (max-width: 24rem) { /* condensed metadata in TaskDetail */ }
This way each pane self-adjusts as the user drags, no JS ResizeObserver needed.

Optional polish: View Transitions API on double-click-reset.
  if (document.startViewTransition) document.startViewTransition(() => setWidth(DEFAULT));
Graceful fallback to instant on older runtimes.

CONSTRAINTS
- Vanilla Extract only; no hex outside tokens.ts.
- No inline style objects beyond setting CSS custom properties via ref.
- Functional helpers, early returns, no forEach.
- Light + dark modes both look intentional; the handle is visible-but-quiet at rest, clearly interactive on hover/focus.
- Window resize must clamp current width into [MIN, container-width - DETAIL_MIN].

ACCEPTANCE
- Dragging the divider resizes TaskBoard and TaskDetail smoothly at 60fps with no React re-renders during the drag (verified via Preact devtools / a profiler check).
- Width persists across app restart.
- Keyboard fully works per APG splitter pattern.
- Container queries kick in on the panes themselves as width crosses thresholds.
- No new lint / typecheck / fallow findings; clippy + rustfmt clean.
- @preact/signals is added as a dependency, with a brief note in AGENTS.md on when to reach for signals vs. Zustand vs. useState.
- No new dependency for the resizer itself (see DECISION: BESPOKE IMPLEMENTATION).
result: Shipped via PR #19 (resizable-pane). Bespoke WorkspaceResizer with PointerEvents + CSS Grid + @preact/signals for ephemeral drag state; committed width persisted via Zustand/Tauri. @preact/signals added with an AGENTS.md note. One open follow-up: divider styling currently reads as a macOS scrollbar (0.5rem wide, fully rounded) — tracked separately.

name: Investigation: batch ingredient evaluation to reduce LLM cost
description:
Goal: add batched Claude ingredient picks that compose existing per-ingredient goldens (ktdpt2fi), reducing LLM calls without duplicating Spoonacular fixtures.

Prerequisite (done): ktdpt2fi — 10 individual *.spoonacular.json + *.expected.json; spoonacular:ingredient:pick:golden passes.

Approach:
- Individual goldens remain source of truth for candidates and acceptable picks
- Batch fixtures only list member slugs (inheritance); expected derived from each member's *.expected.json
- Target batch size: 5 per case; 2 batch cases from the 10 individuals (mixed difficulty)
- New harness: spoonacular:ingredient:pick:batch:golden (Claude only, no Spoonacular)

Questions to answer:
- Prompt/schema for N independent picks in one structured response
- Failure modes (cross-item poisoning, especially no_match items like ti-leaves)
- Token/cost tradeoffs vs per-item calls
- Go/no-go for production batching

Done when all subtasks complete.
result: Batched ingredient pick investigation complete: inheritance-based batch manifests (2×5), pickSpoonacularIngredientCandidatesBatchWithClaude + schema, test:ai:spoonacular:ingredient:pick:batch:golden passes. Design doc with go/no-go in batches/README.md. Finding: batch prompt must match single-item judgment (belacan case). Prod batching deferred pending go/no-go.

name: Show completed tasks in subtask list
description:
In the subtask/task list view, completed (non-archived) tasks should remain visible rather than disappearing. This gives the user a sense of progress and lets them reference recently completed work without losing context.

## Sort order

Tasks render in three groups, top to bottom:

1. **In-progress** (`[>]`) — actively being worked on, at the very top
2. **Incomplete** (`[ ]`) — open work, not yet started
3. **Complete** (`[x]`) — finished work, at the bottom

Within each group, existing priority/ordering rules apply (e.g. priority `-p` value, then creation order). Active work always sits above finished work.

## Visual treatment

Completed tasks should look visibly done — muted color, strikethrough on the title, or similar — to match how the dex CLI renders `[x]` items. They should read as "context, not action" at a glance.

## Out of scope

- **Archived tasks remain hidden.** This change is about completed-but-not-archived only.
- No change to filtering controls, search, or the count badges in headers (revisit separately if needed).
result: Fetch completed tasks via dex list --all --json; sort task lists in_progress → pending → done before priority/name. Done styling already present in TaskList and TaskDetail.

name: Batch pick: golden manifests (2× batch of 5)
description: Add batch fixture manifests referencing existing per-ingredient slugs. Done when: fixtures/golden/batches/ has 2 *.batch.json (5 members each), README documents format, members resolve to *.spoonacular.json.
result: mixed-pantry-5 + hard-and-edge-5 batch manifests; golden README updated.

name: Recipe import: confirm before persist
description:
Curated recipe ingestion: the DB is for **good** data only, not every parse attempt.

## Principle
- **Scan = preview** (Claude reads the page). **Confirm = commit** (writes + enrichment). Until confirm: no Turso food rows, no Spoonacular, no ingredient resolution that mutates state.
- **Human gate:** Greg reviews parsed recipe + ingredients and can say “whoa” — discard, or take the URL to an agent session to add golden fixtures / fix prompts before trying again. Bad parses should become **tests**, not permanent rows.
- **Cost discipline:** Don’t spend Claude or Spoonacular calls on enrichment until the human is happy with the extracted recipe and ingredient list.

## Context (today)
- Extraction calls `resolveIngredientsAgainstDb` during scan (inserts + optional Spoonacular). Recipe row waits for **Create recipe**.
- Food tables were wiped 2026-05-18; fresh start for this workflow.

## Child tasks
Implement confirm-before-persist first (`z3ksyi5w`), then preview/audit UI (`dh34x9uy`), then library UX (source links, markdown, ingredient↔recipes). When extraction quality is wrong, **pause implementation** and work with Greg on goldens — don’t paper over with more auto-insert logic.

## Done when
- Nothing hits DB or Spoonacular until explicit confirm.
- Preview UI makes it easy to judge ingredient lines before commit.
- Subtasks below complete or explicitly deferred with Greg.
result: Import preview: confirm before persist, ingredient lines, source links, markdown audit UI—all shipped and verified in app.

result: Done: M1–M3 shipped (bash, safety, TASKS registry). M4 closed as won't-do—keeping interactive brews.sh as single source of truth.

name: Cloudflare + Hono: slug redirects
description:
Deploy vilos92.com to Cloudflare with Hono worker for path-based redirects.

Requirements:
- GET /:slug — 302 to matching GitHub repo from slug map; no UI on slug paths
- GET / — interim 302 to https://github.com/Vilos92 (username is capital V, unlike vilos92.com domain)
- Single slug map (e.g. projects.json) as source of truth for slugs
- Unknown slug → sensible 404

Stack:
- Wrangler + @cloudflare/vite-plugin
- Hono for routing

Out of scope (separate task): fuzzy-find picker at /

Done when:

- /known-slug redirects to correct repo

- unknown slug returns 404
result: Deployed to Cloudflare: Hono worker, projects.json (52 repos, public/private), slug redirects + fuzzysort fuzzy match (public only), / → GitHub profile interim removed when h39 ships.

name: Full keyboard navigation (vim + arrows, expanding from there)
description:
GOAL
Make gdex fully usable from the keyboard — no mouse required for any common workflow. Start small with task-list navigation and grow outward.

INITIAL SCOPE
- Move up/down through tasks at the current zoom level: j/k and ArrowDown/ArrowUp.
- Move into a task's subtasks (zoom in) and back out (zoom out): h/l or ArrowLeft/ArrowRight (direction to be decided — see open questions).
- Activate the selected task (open / focus the detail pane): Enter.
- Escape: cancel current focus state where it makes sense (e.g. blur a focused row, close a menu — Escape on the agent prompt menu already exists in src/components/TaskAgentPromptMenu/TaskAgentPromptMenu.tsx:52).

That is the v1 surface. Everything else — workspace switching, creating tasks, editing, the new resizer, command palette — comes after we agree the foundation works.

CURRENT STATE
Almost no keyboard handling exists in src/. Single keydown listener in TaskAgentPromptMenu. No hotkey library, no focus management strategy, no roving tabindex, no aria-activedescendant pattern in place. Clean slate.

WORK WITH ME
This task is intentionally not prescriptive past the v1 scope above. Before writing code, propose answers to these and get my sign-off:

1. Library or bespoke? Candidates worth weighing: tinykeys (~600B, no deps, scoped listeners), hotkeys-js (heavier, sequences + scopes), or rolling our own dispatcher. Same heuristic as the resizer task — small + owned is fine if the surface stays simple.

2. Focus management pattern. Two real options for the task list:
   • Roving tabindex — one item has tabindex=0, others tabindex=-1, focus moves with selection. Most native, screen readers love it.
   • aria-activedescendant — the listbox keeps focus, a child id is announced as active. Cleaner for virtualization, slightly fussier for screen readers.
   Pick one and stick to it across all list-like surfaces (tasks, workspaces, future menus).

3. Vim semantics. h/l for zoom in/out feels natural to vim users but conflicts with the left/right convention (left = parent, right = child? or the opposite?). Confirm direction before committing — it has to feel obvious.

4. Where the listeners live. Per-component (composable, easy to scope) vs. a centralized hotkeys layer (single source of truth, easier discoverability). Probably per-component for v1, centralized later if the surface grows.

5. Modal or not. Pure passthrough (j/k always navigate when focus is in the list) vs. modal (a normal/insert split à la real vim). Strong preference: NOT modal for v1 — modes are surprising in a GUI.

6. Discoverability. Do we want a `?` cheatsheet overlay from day one, or land the bindings first and add the overlay as a follow-up?

CONSTRAINTS
- Vanilla Extract for any styling; no inline style objects.
- Functional helpers, early returns, no forEach.
- Bindings must not fire while a text input or contenteditable is focused (standard guard: check event.target).
- Accessibility takes priority over vim purity. Tab order, focus rings, aria-current, aria-selected, role on lists — all correct before we celebrate the bindings.
- Bindings should be defined as data (a small map), not scattered switch statements, so the future cheatsheet can render from the same source.

ACCEPTANCE (v1)
- Can navigate the visible task list with j/k and ArrowUp/ArrowDown; selection follows focus.
- Can zoom into a task's subtasks and back out with the agreed keys.
- Enter activates the selected task.
- Focus ring is visible and obvious; never lost into the void after navigation.
- No regression for mouse users.
- No new lint / typecheck / fallow findings.
- A short note in AGENTS.md on the chosen library/pattern + focus-management approach, so the next surface (workspace list, command palette) follows the same shape.

OUT OF SCOPE (follow-up tasks)
- Workspace switching shortcuts.
- Task creation / edit / delete shortcuts.
- Command palette (Cmd+K).
- Drag-free resizer adjustment (already covered in task hwyhgcu3 via APG splitter keys).
- Cheatsheet overlay (may or may not be in scope — see open question 6).

description:
Goal: finish and harden the ingredient testing/golden workflow.

Scope:

- Define/maintain expected allowlists for Claude pick per ingredient

description:

name: Workspace home panel: details + delete
description: When no task is selected (root view of a workspace), the right panel should show workspace details instead of a placeholder message. Display: workspace name, config file path, and storage directory path — all read-only/non-editable. Include a delete workspace action (with confirmation) that removes it from the registry and returns to the splash screen if no other workspaces remain, or switches to another workspace if one exists. Establish 'workspace' as the user-facing term for what the code currently calls 'project'.
result: WorkspaceHomePanel renders workspace name, config path, and storage path when no task is selected, with a two-step delete confirmation that either returns to the splash screen or activates the next workspace depending on how many remain

name: Rename 'project' to 'workspace' throughout
description: Rename the 'project' concept to 'workspace' everywhere: Rust structs (Project -> Workspace), Tauri commands (add_project -> add_workspace, list_projects -> list_workspaces, etc), TypeScript types and API helpers (projectApi.ts -> workspaceApi.ts, Project -> Workspace), store state (projects -> workspaces, activeProjectId -> activeWorkspaceId), UI copy and component names (ProjectSidebar -> WorkspaceSidebar, etc). The rename is purely mechanical — no behavior changes. Rationale: a workspace contains dex tasks which represent real work items; calling the container a project conflicts with how users think about their actual projects.
result: Renamed project→workspace across Rust and TS. Follow-ups: workspace Zod wire schema (config_path/storage_path→camelCase), task detail child list labeled Tasks not Subtasks. check/typecheck pass.

name: UI: task detail

Breadcrumb navigateToTask syncs list+detail. Per-column scroll (detail + project sidebar body). Collapsible project sidebar with panel icon toggle.

name: Update copy/paste snippets to use 'dex' directly instead of 'gdex' alias
description: The gdex GUI (this project, named 'gdex') currently shows copy/paste snippets that reference the user's personal 'gdex' shell alias. Other users won't have that alias — snippets should invoke 'dex' directly (with appropriate --config / --storage-path flags as needed) so they're portable. Audit all snippet sources in src/ and update accordingly.
result: Agent prompt snippets now invoke dex directly with --config and --storage-path from the active gdex workspace (no legacy gdex CLI wrapper). Workspace object is threaded through TaskDetailQuickPrompts and TaskAgentPromptMenu; TaskDetailQuickPrompts resets via keyed remount on task/workspace change.

name: Agent instruction snippets: design + implementation
description:
The cornerstone feature of gdex: surfacing quick copy/paste instructions for agents, tied to dex operations.

## Locked design (2026-05-23)

**Section title:** Quick prompts

**Location:** Bottom of the right-hand detail panel (below description, result, blockers, subtasks).

**Interaction:** Flat `<select>` — copy-on-select with brief "Copied!" feedback (same pattern as task ID copy). Interpolate `{task_id}` and active workspace name into `gdex <workspace> …` commands.

**v1 snippets (order):**
1. **Start task** — kick off / mark in progress (first item; highest priority)
2. **Mark complete** — `gdex <workspace> complete {task_id} --result "…"`
3. **Delete task** — safe delete with subtask cascade warning

**Deferred:** Update description snippet (cut from v1).

**Follow-up:** Subtask `farzygud` — right-click task list context menu ("Copy start task quick prompt", extensible).

**Still default unless changed:** flat select (not grouped); copy-on-select only (no preview); conditional visibility TBD (e.g. hide start when already in progress, hide complete when done).
result: Quick prompts in detail panel (select + code block + copy), agentPrompts.ts snippets, component reorg and AGENTS conventions.

name: Project registration: explicit config + storage paths
description:
Registering a dex project in gdex binds to an existing dex profile (same as CLI/gdex):

- config_path — user-chosen dex project config file (e.g. ~/.dex/projects/greg/config.toml)
- storage_path — user-chosen task storage directory (e.g. ~/.dex/task-db/greg.jsonl)

Backend:
- Project and add_project take name, config_path, storage_path
- gdex must not create, write, or delete config or task storage files
- DexClient passes both paths to every dex invocation

UI (future, under project management): two pickers for config file and storage directory; validate paths exist before save.

Out of scope: auto-generating config.toml under app data; storage-only single-picker registration.
result: Reverted storage-only experiment. Project and add_project keep name, config_path, storage_path; gdex does not create or delete dex config/storage files. DexClient unchanged (both paths per invoke). UI dual-picker deferred to project-management milestone.

name: Plugins + Rust deps
description: bun tauri add store dialog. Cargo: notify, thiserror; confirm serde/serde_json. Capabilities for dialog. Dex binary resolved at startup via std::process::Command (which dex) with result stored in Tauri managed state — handles macOS PATH gotcha. All dex invocations happen Rust-side via std::process::Command; no shell plugin needed.
result: Added tauri-plugin-store and tauri-plugin-dialog (no shell). Cargo: notify 7, thiserror 2; serde/serde_json confirmed. dialog:default capability for folder picker. Dex path resolved at startup via which dex into DexBinaryPath managed state; fails fast if missing. Validation: check, typecheck, fallow:audit, clippy, fmt:rust:check all pass.

name: Tests: public/private slug routing
description:
Lock in slug visibility rules with fixture-based tests (not only live projects.json).

Cover:
- Exact match: public and private both redirect
- Fuzzy (fuzzysort): public typos resolve; typos near private slugs do not
- resolvePath end-to-end for typo → private → not_found
- findFuzzyPublicProject never returns a private project

Done when: tests use a small Project[] fixture and pass without conditional skips.
result: Fixture-based tests in routing.visibility.test.ts; resolvePathWithProjects for injection. Covers exact public/private, fuzzy public, typo→private 404, fuzzy never returns private.

name: Better / UI: Preact + edge-friendly styling
description:
Upgrade the / hub from bare HTML to a proper renderer and design system (edge-friendly).

Direction:
- Preact for the fuzzy-find hub
- Styling: Vanilla Extract (@vanilla-extract/css + @vanilla-extract/vite-plugin) — zero-runtime, build-time CSS; install with `bun i` (dev deps). Do not use Tailwind for this task.
- Verify Vite+ dev/build and Cloudflare Worker bundle after wiring VE

Requirements:
- Polished, minimal UI consistent with a personal project hub
- Works in Cloudflare Workers + Vite+ dev/build story
- Shares projects map with slug redirect routes (reuse `publicProjects` / `searchPublicProjects`)

Depends on: h39pjguq (Fuzzy-find hub at /) — done on main.

Done when:
- / uses Preact + Vanilla Extract
- Dev and production deploy look intentional, not starter-template
result: Hub uses Preact + Vanilla Extract with prerendered /, custom dark UI, combobox a11y, and shared projects catalog with slug redirects. vp build passes (Worker + client).

name: vilos92.com
description:
Umbrella for vilos92.com repo work. Use child tasks for concrete efforts.

Vision:
- vilos92.com/ — simple site with fuzzy find across Greg's GitHub projects
- vilos92.com/<slug> — go straight to the matching GitHub project (short path → repo)

Notes:
- Super minimal surface area; routing/redirect hub, not a full app
- Child tasks: implementation milestones, config (slug → repo map), deploy, etc.
result: vilos92.com hub live: fuzzy find at /, slug redirects and API resolve, Preact + Vanilla Extract UI on Cloudflare Workers.

name: Restyle WorkspaceResizer so it doesn't read as a scrollbar
description:
FOLLOW-UP TO hwyhgcu3

PROBLEM
The current WorkspaceResizer handle visually reads as a macOS overlay scrollbar:
- src/components/WorkspaceResizer/workspaceResizer.css.ts
- width: '0.5rem' (~8px) + borderRadius: '4px' (= half the width → fully rounded pill)
- alignSelf: 'stretch' (full-height vertical bar) in the gutter
- subtle palette.border / palette.borderDark fill at rest

That silhouette is indistinguishable from a scrollbar at rest, so the affordance is unclear until you happen to hover and see col-resize.

GOAL
Keep the resizer present and discoverable, but visually distinct from a scrollbar. The hit area can stay generous (~0.5rem); the rendered indicator should not.

IDEAS TO EXPLORE (pick one, not all)
- Thin vertical rule (1–2px) centered in a wider transparent hit area, rather than a rounded pill that fills the gutter.
- Center grip dots / short hash marks (•••) that only appear on hover/focus.
- Keep a hairline at rest; thicken + tint on hover/focus/data-dragging.
- Use a non-pill border-radius (e.g. 1px or 0) so it stops mimicking the scrollbar shape.

CONSTRAINTS (carry-over from hwyhgcu3)
- Vanilla Extract only; tokens via palette / tokens.ts.
- Light + dark both look intentional.
- Quiet at rest, clearly interactive on hover/focus, obvious during drag (data-dragging="true").
- Don't shrink the actual pointer hit area — only the visual indicator.

ACCEPTANCE
- Side-by-side with the system scrollbar, the divider is not mistakable for one.
- Hover / focus / drag states still feel native.
- No new lint/typecheck findings.

name: Milestone 1: shell + paths consistent
description: Decide Bash vs POSIX sh; fix redirection portability; use /Users/greg.linscheid, avoid hardcoded /Users/...
result: Set brews.sh to bash, fixed portable brew check redirection, and removed hardcoded home path in .zprofile writes.

name: Ingredient page: detail + recipes using it
description:
## Goal
After data is **confirmed** into the library, navigate ingredient → recipes that use it (audit curated graph, not scan-time behavior).

## Today
- Flat `/ingredient` list only; no detail route or “recipes using this” query.

## Work
1. Route e.g. `/ingredient/$ingredientId` + server fn listing recipes with that `ingredient_id`.
2. Links from list → detail → recipe pages.

## Acceptance
- Used ingredients show recipe links; orphans show empty state.
result: Ingredient layout with Outlet, /ingredient/$id detail + recipes list; links from index and recipe detail.

name: v0 integration pass
description:
v0 integration pass: validate quick prompts against a committed dex fixture.

## Goals

1. **Committed test dex workspace** — Add a comprehensive dex config + task storage fixture in this repo (config.toml, tasks.jsonl tree, and brief README for how gdex/gdex registers it). The dataset should exercise real-world shapes: parent/child tasks, pending / in-progress / completed, subtasks, and any states needed for quick-prompt visibility rules.

2. **Quick-prompt workflow validation** — For each quick prompt in `agentPrompts.ts` (view, start, complete, delete), run through the exact `gdex <workspace> …` commands the UI copies. Confirm a real agent following the pasted text can complete the intended workflow without ambiguity or missing steps. Fix prompt copy or fixture tasks when something does not match.

3. **App smoke (secondary)** — Register the fixture workspace in gdex, browse list/detail, copy prompts, and confirm Rust `DexClient` + UI behave against the fixture paths.

## Out of scope (unless we expand later)

- Mutations from the gdex UI (still read-only v0).
- Automated Vitest/e2e unless we explicitly add them in this task.

## Deliverables

- Fixture under repo (path TBD in implementation).
- Short validation notes or checklist in the task result when complete.
- Any prompt text fixes discovered during the pass.

## Notes

- Parent milestone: gdex (ia40wzb6). Builds on quick prompts (oobeapzz).
- Do not mark complete until prompts + fixture have been exercised end-to-end.

name: Milestone 2: remove eval + add safety/guards
description: Replace eval-based command execution; add strict mode and sensible traps; guard steps that assume tools (pip3/pipx/etc).
result: Removed eval-based installs by switching prompt helper to execute command+args; added strict bash mode; added shell-only helper for pipelines/&&; added python3 guard for copyparty install.

name: DexClient (Rust)
description: src-tauri/src/dex_client.rs: shells out to dex for reads only in v0. Each call passes --config and --storage-path for the active project. Reads: dex list --json (flat array, reconstruct tree client-side via parent_id/children fields) and dex show <id> --json for detail. Define a Task struct matching the JSON shape. DexError via thiserror. Expose: list_tasks, show_task.
result: Implemented dex_client.rs: read-only DexClient shells out to resolved dex binary for list_tasks and show_task with Task/DexError types.

name: Cleanup and polish
description: Full pass over the app since initial creation—UI, code, consistency, dead ends. Greg will review files manually first; scope and subtasks to be added as we go.
result: PR #1 merged to main: lib refactor (@/ aliases, slug-fuzzy, project-search), CI fallow audit, AGENTS validation loop, sync-projects-json skill; CodeRabbit feedback (skill fallow step, text fence, deterministic slug-fuzzy fixtures). vp check, vp test, fallow:audit passed.

name: Introduce Zustand for shared UI state
description: Add Zustand and migrate app state out of hooks and prop drilling. Store shape: projects, activeProjectId, tasks (for active project), selectedTaskId, zoomedTaskId (drill-down context). Move the tasks-changed event subscription into the store so it is not tied to component lifecycle. Clean up ProjectsLayout — it should read from the store rather than threading state through props. Do this before or alongside the detail panel milestone so selectedTaskId is available to both the task list and detail panel without lifting state. Also introduce Zod schemas for all data crossing the Tauri boundary (Task, Project, and any other invoke return types). Schemas live in src/schemas/ (e.g. src/schemas/task.ts, src/schemas/project.ts) using a Schemas. section comment per AGENTS.md file layout conventions. Parse at the call site in taskApi.ts and projectApi.ts — if Rust sends back a shape we don't expect, we catch it immediately at the edge rather than letting bad data propagate into the store.
result: Zod schemas parse Tauri invoke payloads at taskApi/projectApi edges. Zustand appStore holds projects, active project, tasks, loading/error, and task nav; initTasksListener and reloadTasks live in the store. Hooks/views read from store—ProjectMain and ProjectsLayout no longer thread seven props.

name: Import: confirm before DB + Spoonacular
description:
## Goal
Single commit path after human confirm. **Zero** DB writes and **zero** Spoonacular/Claude enrichment during scan.

## Why (read parent `jvprxjqg`)
Turso should only receive imports Greg has audited. Spoonacular + Claude candidate-pick are expensive and pointless if the parse is wrong — run them **only after confirm**, for ingredients actually being saved.

## Today
- Scan: `resolveIngredientsAgainstDb` inserts `ingredient` rows + may Spoonacular-enrich (`ingredient-resolution.ts`).
- Confirm-ish: **Create recipe** already inserts `recipe` + `recipe_ingredient` only (but ingredient IDs must already exist from scan).
- Escape hatch: manual enrich on `/ingredient` list stays.

## Work
1. Scan/extract: structured preview only; optional **read-only** match to existing ingredients for display labels (no insert).
2. Confirm: resolve/create ingredients → Spoonacular for **new** rows only (if key set) → insert recipe + joins; one server fn / transaction preferred.
3. UI: confirm vs discard; drop “new ingredient” badges that implied DB state during scan.
4. **If Greg flags a bad site during this work:** stop coding around it — capture URL + HTML fixture under `test/fixtures/recipes/golden/`, adjust prompts/schemas, run `bun run test:ai:recipe:url:golden`. Collaborate; don’t auto-insert workarounds.

## Acceptance
- `ingredient` row count unchanged after scan, before confirm.
- Spoonacular not called during scan.
- After confirm: same end state as today when key present.
result: Merged PR #5: preview-only extract, confirm-time resolve + Spoonacular, duplicate URL check, transactional persist (ingredient match + recipe insert).

name: Master recipe catalog for goldens
description: After fixture reorg (and likely co-located docs for how to create/run fixtures), add a single master catalog (recipe list) — format TBD: recipe, parse-difficulty score, parsing notes. Catalog may list more recipes than we have fixtures; every fixture MUST reference an entry in this catalog. Bootstrap: scan repo for existing recipe URLs near current fixtures and seed the catalog before adding new URLs from Greg.
result: Added test/fixtures/recipes/catalog.yaml (7 entries: 4 golden, 3 candidate) with parseDifficulty 1–5 and parsingNotes. README documents fields and scale. Golden expected.json uses catalogId; recipe-url-golden.ts resolves canonicalUrl from catalog and validates goldenFixtureBase.

name: App icon for the built binary
description:
FOLLOW-UP TO 4dgaqbbb (macOS release workflow)

GOAL
Replace the default Tauri app icon with a dedicated gdex icon so the built .app / .dmg / Dock tile / Finder thumbnail are unmistakably ours.

COLLABORATION NOTE
Greg will work with the agent to define / supply the source icon. Do NOT generate or guess an icon — wait for him to either:
- Provide a source asset (PNG/SVG at sufficient resolution, ideally ≥1024×1024), or
- Pair on iterating a design (vibe, palette, motif), then export.

WORK
Once a source asset exists:
- Drop it into src-tauri/icons/ (or wherever Tauri convention places it for this project — verify before moving things).
- Regenerate the full icon set with `pnpm tauri icon <path-to-source>` (or equivalent) so all the platform-specific sizes (.icns, .ico, multiple PNG sizes) are produced from one source of truth.
- Confirm tauri.conf.json bundle.icon[] points at the regenerated files.
- Verify in a local build: Dock tile, About window, Finder icon, .dmg volume icon all show the new artwork in light + dark backgrounds.

ACCEPTANCE
- A built .app on macOS shows the gdex icon in Finder, Dock, ⌘-Tab switcher, and About panel.
- The .dmg from the release workflow shows the icon for the app bundle inside it.
- Source asset is committed (or a clear note in the repo points to where it lives).
- No leftover Tauri default icon files referenced from tauri.conf.json.
